items.title: 3.4% in 12 months: what are US consumers bracing for?
items.summary: The Federal Reserve (Fed) Bank of New York's Survey of Consumer Expectations (SCE) for March showed one-year-ahead inflation expectations jumping to 3.4%, up 0.4 percentage points from February's 3.0% reading.

items.title: US: Oil shock complicates Fed path – MUFG
items.summary: MUFG’s Head of Research Derek Halpenny argues that rising Oil and gasoline prices from the Middle East conflict are set to push US inflation higher, with March CPI expected to jump. He highlights a sharp rise in ISM Services prices paid and weakening employment indices.

items.title: Oil: Supply shock supports $90–100 well into 2027 – TD Securities
items.summary: TD Securities’ commodity team argues that prolonged disruption in the Persian Gulf and blocked flows through the Strait of Hormuz leave Oil poised for another sharp leg higher.

items.title: Iran cuts off direct communications with US – WSJ
items.summary: The Wall Street Journal reported on Tuesday that Iran has cut off direct communications with the United States (US) following President Donald Trump's threat to destroy Iran's whole civilization.

items.title: NZD: RBNZ seen on hold with dovish tone – ING
items.summary: ING’s Chris Turner expects the Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) to leave rates unchanged at 2.25% with no new forecasts and limited guidance, as markets price only modest tightening this year.

items.title: Brent: Futures curve signals temporary price spike – Deutsche Bank
items.summary: Henry Allen at Deutsche Bank says Brent crude Oil above $100 has not triggered a 1970s‑style shock because markets still discount a short conflict and lower prices ahead.

items.title: SEK: Softer inflation may delay Riksbank hikes – TD Securities
items.summary: TD Securities analysts note Swedish CPIF and CPIF ex-Energy inflation for March surprised sharply to the downside, driven mainly by weaker Food and Recreation, Sport & Culture prices, partly offset by petrol.

items.title: Fed's Williams: Iran war impact to drive up headline inflation
items.summary: Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams told Bloomberg on Tuesday that the impact of the Iran war will drive up headline inflation.

items.title: RBNZ: Constrained as energy shock bites – BBH
items.summary: Brown Brothers Harriman’s (BBH) Elias Haddad expects the Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) to keep the OCR at 2.25%, with Governor Breman set to update growth and inflation projections.

items.title: S&P 500: Resilience with limited stagflation fallout – Deutsche Bank
items.summary: Deutsche Bank’s Henry Allen argues that the S&P 500’s modest pullback versus past Oil shocks reflects markets pricing a short conflict, resilient macro data and still‑dovish central banks.

items.title: USD: Dovish Fed risks and ceasefire repricing – Societe Generale
items.summary: Societe Generale’s Kit Juckes discusses Robin Brooks’ view that the Dollar looks significantly overvalued versus G10 rate differentials and could fall sharply on a ceasefire, with Oil tumbling and safe-haven flows reversing.

items.title: AUD/NZD: Diverging flows and policy risks – BNY
items.summary: BNY’s Head of Markets Macro Strategy Bob Savage highlights a growing divergence between the New Zealand Dollar (NZD) and Australian Dollar (AUD) as markets favor currencies backed by real assets.
